transcript.clean_text: always expect the unexpected on level temperatures today soaring to near 40 celsius according to some indicators virtually no wind and a day that looked on paper at least potentially for one for the breakaway but how big would it be would the sprinters get involved perhaps the gc men we just didn't know one thing was certain it would be very quick on the descent at the beginning of the day with the two lumps at the end defining the story in the end we had a brave breakaway devolve was up there for 82 our citroen we had yetzerbal as well from burgos joined by eturia from oscar beric van hills army and chad harger well the chances of the breakaway making it were quite slim mainly because the chasing duties being put in by ue team emirates as a result dsm called chad harger out of the break said it's not worth it they're not going to make it so he was first to disappear but the break held firm and indeed the margin was around a minute and a half in three quarters until this as uae team emirates were pushing on into that gap a big touch of wheels and plenty taken down not least jumbo vismas primos roglich also hampered inies grenadiers likewise mowi star bahrain victorious and confidence so the break pushed on last roll of the dice came from mactin van hills from lotto sudow and the gap was a very very modest one with just under 26 kilometers to go well as one break was caught it always begs the question whether another one will be formed and because of the pace that's been put in in the day by uae team emirates there were riders all over the parkour and the heat was taking its toll a combativity award is one thing but there was a stage victory up for grabs uae really burnt all of their matches in the chase they were helped on occasion by intermarche wanti who were honoring the red jersey on the back of old christian hiking but really it was about who would survive the day rather than exploit it jaconi went off the front he had good company as well vine got involved likewise bar day and and now so we had a new break of four riders with just over 20 kilometers to go we still had a big climb as well and off its descent it was high paced would the peloton have made a mistake or we'd find out very very late on half a minute advantage around that with 5.2 kilometers to go and a new team at the front it was team bike exchange matthews had survived trenton was still involved as well they remained the favorites but could they exploit the day finally a big coming together just about at the flam rouge big left-hander when the road would suddenly open up and open up certainly did magnus chord on the wheel of jen's curcular a perfect lead out for him baggioli the big challenge matthews and trent in just going for minor places but who would get it on the line it would be on the throw surely it couldn't be magnus caught again after all the exploits yesterday but it was you can't keep a good man down but magnus court nielson is a very good man indeed winner of stage six and then on stage 12 the day prior he'd gone very very long to lose out within 200 meters of the line he picked himself up and started all over again and won the day magnus quarter has a badgioli matthews trentine and kron with grushartner up there soto and rugby great day for a great man i'm chrissy nike good finish for him as well stayed safe remains in the lead for yet another day 58 seconds clear of gimme pretty much in the front group as well along with the other favorites so into marsha wanti get to show off the leaders jersey once again on stage 13 bound to be unlucky for somebody it is after all the longest stage of this year's welter and potentially the hottest as well if temperatures are predicted to rise even above today's amazing tally no climbs to speak of not exactly flat but it'll probably feel like that out there certainly it will be punishing under the sun so who will take advantage of the question we shall have to wait and see you
